title: cyrtomium nephrolepioides
intro: Cyrtomium nephrolepioides, also known as the Osmundopteris nephrolepioides or the Wood fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health issues. The plant has been used to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and even treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, offering numerous health benefits. Cyrtomium nephrolepioides can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
